description: This scenario models a severe macroeconomic crisis characterized by financial instability, market collapse, fiscal stress, and cascading effects across governance, social stability, and cyber infrastructure.

cyber_stability.description: Indirect impact due to reduced investment and operational constraints

ai_governance.description: Slower regulatory development and constrained AI deployment due to economic pressure

legal_coherence.description: Emergency fiscal policies and regulatory inconsistencies affect legal stability

economic_resilience.description: Severe contraction, financial system instability, and liquidity crisis

social_stability.description: Unemployment, inequality, and public unrest increase

shock_propagation.propagation_pattern: economic collapse → social instability → legal fragmentation → technological slowdown

legal_coherence.impact: Policy inconsistency due to emergency economic measures

decision_integrity_score.impact: System degrades from stable to moderate-high risk

recovery_dynamics.recovery_pattern: financial stabilization → policy correction → economic recovery → social normalization

uncertainty_model.notes: Outcome variability depends on policy response speed and global economic environment

simulation_notes.purpose: Evaluates systemic impact of economic crises across sovereign domains
simulation_notes.interpretation: Highlights economic system as primary driver of cross-domain instability
simulation_notes.usage: Macroeconomic policy modeling, financial stability analysis, and crisis response planning
